2) master price sensitivity -> master prop firms. prop firms are a game of precision.

always remember, luck can be optimized. 

if there's dumb luck, there is also smart luck.

increasing 'rulebook complexity' is typically always worse off for the trader. 

it actually increases the gamification of the model. playing 'optimally' becomes a lot more important. 

more rules --> the further you get from trading.

got a great dm yesterday asking what really changed before the payouts became consistent.

it was realizing i wasn't just trading the market. i was trading inside a specific game: the prop firm system.

i stopped trying to force my personal "trading style" onto their rules.

and

The math works like this: 

50k Account
Profit Target: $3,000
Drawdown: $2,000
Cost: $50
PT/DD: 1.5
Cost (with activation): $200

Cost per $ of Drawdown Buffer (Lower = Better Efficiency): $200 / $2,000 DD = $0.10

100k Account
Profit Target: $6,000
Drawdown: $3,000
Cost: $100

I only traded the 150k challenges on @Topstep 

I prefer 150k to the 50k evaluations. there are times where the smaller evaluations are better deals considering the risk to payout ratios. 

I prefer/like the added capacity. If I am playing a game where I have a statistical

The general advice I give traders that are centered around 'milestone thinking' is to actually ignore them all together. 

milestones have nothing to do with your edge. they are arbitrary endpoints. 

the issue I see is traders trade for the milestone rather than the chart in

Prop firms found a way to make trading feel like a video game. 

evaluations and challenges act as "levels" to the game. 

Pass Evaluation -> level up to unlock funding
Pass Funding -> level up to unlock payout 

at each milestone, you FEEL like you are winning. but in reality,

im back. been about a month. 

what did I miss ?
in anything I do, I always learn from the best and treat their work with the highest respect.

i still believe that prop firms are one of the greatest ways to scale your trading capital. 

4 figs -> aim for 5 figs 
5 figs -> aim for 6 figs

it's hard. but its not unachievable to someone who plays their cards right.
being aware and understanding on a fundamental level what you are playing is key.

ON TRADE OUTCOMES

there are 2 outcomes when you take a trade: you either WIN or LOSE. 

you can't always win every trade. it's impossible to win 100% of your trades (with a large enough, statistically significant sample size). so, you are going to have losses. 

there's 2 ways
NEVER STOP HUNTING.

NO ONE IS COMING TO SAVE YOU. 

i've seen friends and traders come and go over the years. seen traders multiply their accounts, only to give it all back. seen many times traders run up their account 5x/10x/20x even, then lose it all months later. what comes easy often goes easy.

if you hit ___ ($$) per month, you’re happy. 

what's your number ?
i dont have a particular number in mind personally. just long term goals to get to to. don't really like the notion of a consistent goal like "i need to make x amount this month".

the people that "make it" in trading (and in prop firms) is very, very small. you are against the system. it's an incredibly slim number. you face "restaurant level odds" of failure. i'm

on a random baseball game, maybe you risk 0.5 units.
